import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    # Check for duplicates
    from collections import Counter
    cnt = Counter(a)
    if max(cnt.values()) > 1:
        print("YES")
        return
    
    # No duplicates, compute permutation parity
    # sorted array is 1,2,...,n (since values are 1..n and all distinct)
    # permutation p where p[i] = position where a[i] should go
    # Actually: sorted(a)[j] should come from position where a has that value
    # p[i] = sorted_position of a[i], i.e., a[i]-1 (since values are 1..n distinct)
    # We need to sort a, so element at position i (0-indexed) with value a[i] needs to go to position a[i]-1
    
    visited = [False] * n
    num_cycles = 0
    for i in range(n):
        if not visited[i]:
            num_cycles += 1
            j = i
            while not visited[j]:
                visited[j] = True
                j = a[j] - 1  # a[j] is the value, it should go to position a[j]-1
    
    parity = (n - num_cycles) % 2
    print("YES" if parity == 0 else "NO")

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()
